PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jul-18-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jason Louie, via Zoom, for and with Charlie Y.C. Chou, Petitioner; Emilie Calhoun, via Zoom, for Mindy Chou, Objector.

The court points to the following defects in notice: notice was not properly provided via the mandatory DE-120 as item one (1) of the form only identifies the amendment; notice was dated Jul-01-2022 which was less than fifteen (15) days prior to this hearing; and notice was addressed to Mr. Chou care of Mr. Louie as his attorney. Mr. Louie waives notice on the record. The court offers its tentative ruling to take the petition for approval of status of administration under submission with the intent to grant and deny the Petition for Instructions, etc., without prejudice as it does not harm the estate to allow additional time for administration. Mr. Louie, Mr. Chou, and Ms. Calhoun presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the Petition for Instructions and estate tax matters.

After additional discussion, the matters are taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Cecilia Castellanos. Examiner: Sandra Hilton. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Off Calendar Jan-13-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jason Louie appeared by zoom for and with Charlie Chou by zoom.

The court noted that Ms. Calhoun was not present. Mr. Louie stated that 668 pages of documents were provided, that they had gone back and forth, and asked the court to grant the accounting. The Petition for Settlement of First Account is under submission with the court's intent to grant the petition.

Judge: Vedica Puri. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
Off Calendar Nov-01-2021 Continued To Dec-06-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jason Louie, via ZOOM, for Charlie Chou, via ZOOM, Administrator; Emily Calhoun, via ZOOM, for Mindy Chou, Beneficiary.

Ms. Calhoun reports that she recently received papers from Ms. Louie and believes this may help resolve this matter. Ms. Calhoun mentions issues regarding a bill from a tax attorney and a report to the IRS. Ms. Calhoun advises that she filed an objection and a request to appoint a professional fiduciary. The court reminds Ms. Calhoun that the only matter before the court today is the accounting. The court asks Mr. Louie regarding the IRS bill. Mr. Louie reports that the bill is from 2020 and advises that he referred Ms. Calhoun to the CPA for additional information and that he mentions the bill in a declaration he filed with the court. Mr. Chou affirms that there is nothing nefarious going on and that Mindy has been provided with several opportunities to question the CPA but that she did not do so.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Settlement of First Account is continued to Dec-06-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Apr-16-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emilie Calhoun, via ZOOM, counsel for Mindy Chou, Objector; Jason Louie, via ZOOM, for Charlie Chou, Administrator, via ZOOM.

At the outset, the court queries Mr. Louie regarding what date the 2020 taxes were filed. Mr. advises that they are not yet due. Mr. Chou advises that they have not yet been filed. The court advises that it is inclined to grant the petition but to order a two (2) year accounting under probate code section 10950 from the date of decedent's death up to JUN-23-2020. The court requests that the two year accounting be submitted for filing by MAY-03-2021 with a status date on MAY-17-2021.

The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Robert Woods; Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-21-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jason Louie for and with Charlie Chou (Petitioner).
Emilie Calhoun for Mindy Chew (Petitioner).

Ms. Calhoun stated that she got a letter with a settlement proposal and that there is no effort to move towards a Professional Fiduciary. Mr. Louie stated that Mindy's petition is not complete and therefore, not before the Court. The Court noted that the Siblings are not getting along and asks if it wouldn't be better to have a neutral party in place. Mr. Louie stated that his Client has priority and that at this point they are not interested in having a Professional Fiduciary. Ms. Calhoun stated that they all agree that the Mother died in Taiwan, that it is a lack of trust and that her Client is concerned about the taxes not being paid. Ms. Chew stated that the Taiwan procedure is each party having their own attorney, that the Taiwanese proceeding is being held up, that Taiwan can not levy taxes and that they have worked cooperatively in the Taiwanese proceeding. Both Petitions for Letters of Administration are under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Anasse. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-21-2018 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-19-2018 Continued To Dec-17-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jason Louie for and with Charlie Chou (Petitioner).
Emilie Calhoun for Mindy Chou (Petitioner).

The Court noted that there are competing petitions and asked the Parties if they can agree on a professional fiduciary. Mr. Louie stated that there have been no settlement discussions at this point and that he is prepared to argue. Ms. Calhoun stated that she is willing to consider anyone they propose. Counsel reported that there are assets in Taiwan, that the will has not been found and that the safe deposit box has not been opened. Counsel also spoke of whether this estate would be the ancillary proceeding, whether the Taiwan estate is significantly smaller, whether or not there is any urgency to act on this matter until things get going in Taiwan, no one being appointed to handle the assets, whether or not the majority of the assets are in San Francisco, whether Ms. Calhoun's petition is properly before the Court, Ms. Calhoun's Client nominating a third party, Mr. Louie's Client having priority, complete mistrust and hostility between the siblings, whether the Brother is suitable to serve, the Decedent's address being in Taiwan and being different in both petitions, etc. Both Petitions for Letters of Administration are continued to December 17, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to meet and confer to discuss and see if they can agree on a neutral professional fiduciary.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Santiago. Not reported.
